Moxie's product recommendation:
Moxie Ditch the Frizz Trio consisting of:
1. Gentle Cleansing Shampoo
2. Ultra Hydraitng Conditioner
3. Frizz Fighting Hair Serum
Comments:
The DTFT routine is recommended for people with fine hair who want to reduce frizz and enhance their natural texture. It is not suitable for those with thicker or coarser hair, as the lightweight formulations may not provide enough moisture for their needs.

Why fine hair specifically: Heavy or overly moisturizing products weigh fine hair down, causing it to fall flat and feel limp rather than bouncy and defined. Every product in the DTFT routine is intentionally lightweight to avoid this, giving fine hair the right amount of moisture without sacrificing volume or texture.

Shampoo: Gently cleanses product buildup and impurities from the scalp without stripping or being harsh on the hair strands.
Conditionerr: A thick, mask-like conditioner formulated with shea butter. Deeply conditions and softens hair, delivering rich hydration during the wash.
Frizz Fighting Hair Serum: A lightweight, milky serum applied after washing. Targets the root cause of frizz (dryness) by deeply hydrating the hair shaft without weighing it down or leaving residue.

Cheat sheet: 

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: frizz, fine hair, flat hair, limp hair, or wanting to enhance natural texture.
2. Do not recommend if the customer has thick, coarse, or low-porosity hair that needs heavier moisture.
3. If a customer asks why their hair feels weighed down after using this routine, check whether they are over-applying the serum or conditioner or using it on damp / dry hair, all products are supposed to be used on wet hair

Moxie's product recommendation:
If goal is:
Path A. Wave definition+taming frizz, use:
1. Weightless Leave-In Conditioner
2 Flexi Styling Serum Gel

Path B. Managing frizz+dryness, use:
1. Frizz fighting hair serum
Comments:
The Wavy Routine should be used when the goal is to have more frizz control and longer hold. The Wightless Leave In Conditioner will enhance texture by providing hydration and reducing frizz to bring out the natural waves and the serum gel will help with holding the waves together.

Cheat sheet:

1. Recommend Path A when a customer mentions: wanting defined waves, longer hold, frizz with styling goals, or enhancing their wave pattern.
2. Recommend Path B when a customer mentions: frizz, dryness, or wanting a simple non-styling routine.
3. If a customer wants both hold and deep hydration, guide them toward Path A, the Leave-In Conditioner addresses dryness while the Serum Gel handles hold and definition.
4. Do not recommend the Flexi Styling Serum Gel alone, it works best layered over the Leave-In Conditioner for optimal wave definition and frizz control.

Moxie's product recommendation:
A. Hydrorepair Routine:
1. Hyaluronic acid shampoo
2. Hyaluronic acd conditioner
3. Hyaluronic acid serum
Comments:
The HydroRepair routine is recommended for people with hair that has been damaged through excessive chemical treatments, colouring or heatstyling and they want to reduce frizz and enhance their natural texture and make hair more hydrated and treat damage. It is not suitable for those with virgin hair or people who have never gotten chemical treatments to their hair and don't heat style frequently, as the formulation may not work for them and might weigh their hair down.

Why chemically treated or frequently heat styled hair specifically: The hydrorepair routine is powered by hyaluronic acid and bioactive peptides. The hyaluronic acid reactivates the hydration pathways while the bioactive peptides rebuild the hair's lipid barrier making jhair hydrated and stronger. 

Hyaluronic Acid Repairing Shampoo: Gently cleanses while replenishing lost moisture and reinforcing your hair’s natural barrier.
Conditionerr: A high-performance, protein-enriched formula that restores moisture balance, strengthens weakened strands, and smooths the cuticle.Frizz Fighting Hair Serum: A deeply reparative leave-in that hydrates, smooths, and protects against heat and environmental stressors

Cheat sheet: 

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: damage, heat styling, colour treatment, chemical straightening, protein treatment, smoothening treatment, straightening treatment. 
2. Do not recommend if the customer has natural hair that has never been chemically treated and doesnt heat style frequently
3. If a customer asks why their hair feels weighed down after using this routine, check whether they are over-applying the serum or conditioner or using it on damp / dry hair, all products are supposed to be used on wet hair

Moxie's product recommendation:
Hydrorepair Duo+Curly Vibe Setter Duo:
1. Hyaluronic acid repairing shampoo
2. Hyaluronic acid repairing conditioner
3. Super defining curl cream
4. Flexi styling serum
Comments:
This combined routine is specifically recommended for people with curly hair that is also colour-treated or damaged. It addresses two needs simultaneously: repairing and hydrating compromised hair while also defining and setting natural curls. This is the go to routine when a customer's hair concerns include both damage / colour treatment history and curl enhancement goals.

Phase 1: Repair + Condition (HydroRepair Duo)
Shampoo, conditioner: Infused with Hyaluronic Acid and Bioactive peptides, this duo works to actively reverse visible signs of damage caused by colouring, restoring softness, strength, and moisture back into the hair

Phase 2: Curl Definition + Hold (Curly Vibe Setter Duo)

Curling cream, serum gel: Enhances the natural curl pattern and texture, bringing out the curls while keeping them soft and defined rather than crunchy or stiff and locks the curls in place and holds them together for longer-lasting definition and frizz control throughout the day.

Cheat sheet: 

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: colour-treated hair, damaged hair, curly hair, lack of curl definition, frizz, or dryness due to chemical treatments.
2. This routine is specifically designed for the intersection of damage concerns + curl styling goals both must be present for this to be the right recommendation.
3. If a customer has curly hair but no damage or colour history, direct them to a curl-focused routine without the repair element.
4. If a customer has colour-treated or damaged hair but no curls, direct them to the HydroRepair Routine without the Curly Vibe Setter products.
5. The Curling Cream and Serum Gel work as a pair: do not recommend one without the other, as the cream defines while the gel holds, and both are needed for complete curl setting

Moxie's product recommendation:
If goal is:
Path A. Damage repair for coloured hair use the Hydrorepair Trio consisting of:
1. Hyaluronic acid shampoo
2. Hyaluronic acid conditioner
3. Hyaluronic acid serum

Path B: If goal is to define waves use Wavy Vibe Setter Duo consisting of:
1. Leave in conditioner
2. Flexi styling serum gel
Comments:
People with wavy hair who have colour-treated or damaged hair > Path A
People with wavy hair who want to define and hold their natural wave pattern > Path B
People with wavy, colour-treated or damaged hair who also want wave definition > Both paths combined WITHOUT the Hyaluronic Acid Hair Serum 

Cheatsheet: 

1. Recommend Path A only when the customer mentions: colour-treated hair, chemical damage, dryness from colouring, brittle or weakened strands, or wanting to restore hair health with no mention of styling or wave definition goals.
2. Recommend Path B only when the customer mentions: wanting defined waves, frizz control, enhancing natural wave texture, or needing hold with no mention of damage or colour treatment.
3. Recommend both paths together when the customer has colour-treated or damaged hair and wants to define their waves > Path A handles repair during the wash routine while Path B handles wave styling post-wash.
4. Path A and Path B are independent: neither requires the other to work. Only combine them when both concerns are explicitly present.
5. Within Path B, the Leave-In Conditioner and Flexi Styling Serum Gel must always be recommended together, the Leave-In sets the hydration and wave base while the Serum Gel locks the pattern in place. Do not recommend one without the other.
6. If Path A and B are recommended together, it must be specified that the Hyaluronic Acid Serum should be avoided

Moxie's product recommendation:
If hair goal is damage repair: Hydrorepair Routine
A. Hyaluronic acid shampoo
B. Hyaluronic acid conditioner
C. Hyaluronic acid serum
Comments:
The HydroRepair Essentials Trio is a complete three-step wash and care routine designed to cleanse, strengthen, and protect hair. It is the all-in-one solution for anyone whose primary concern is achieving stronger, healthier, and deeply hydrated hair.

Who it's for
1. People with colour-treated, damaged, or chemically processed hair
2. Anyone experiencing dryness, frizz, or weakened strands
3. People looking for a simple but comprehensive repair routine without needing separate styling products
4. Those whose hair needs protection from heat and environmental damage on an ongoing basis

Cheat sheet: 
1. Recommend this trio when a customer mentions: damaged hair, colour-treated hair, dryness, frizz, heat styling concerns, environmental damage, or wanting an easy all-in-one repair routine.
2. If a customer only wants one or two products, guide them toward the full trio by explaining that the serum seals in the benefits of the shampoo and conditioner — without it, moisture may not be fully locked in.
3. Do not position this as a styling routine: its strength is in repair, hydration, and protection, not texture definition or hold.

The hyaluronic acid reactivates the hydration pathways while the bioactive peptides rebuild the hair's lipid barrier making jhair hydrated and stronger.

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: damage, heat styling, colour treatment, chemical straightening, protein treatment, smoothening treatment, straightening treatment.

3. If a customer asks why their hair feels weighed down after using this routine, check whether they are over-applying the serum or conditioner or using it on damp / dry hair, all products are supposed to be used on wet hair

Moxie's product recommendation:
A. Hydrorepair Routine:
1. Hyaluronic acid shampoo
2. Hyaluronic acd conditioner
3. Hyaluronic acid serum
Comments:
The HydroRepair routine is recommended for people with hair that has been damaged through excessive chemical treatments, colouring or heatstyling and they want to reduce frizz and enhance their natural texture and make hair more hydrated and treat damage. It is not suitable for those with virgin hair or people who have never gotten chemical treatments to their hair and don't heat style frequently, as the formulation may not work for them and might weigh their hair down.

Why chemically treated or frequently heat styled hair specifically: The hydrorepair routine is powered by hyaluronic acid and bioactive peptides. The hyaluronic acid reactivates the hydration pathways while the bioactive peptides rebuild the hair's lipid barrier making jhair hydrated and stronger. 

Hyaluronic Acid Repairing Shampoo: Gently cleanses while replenishing lost moisture and reinforcing your hair’s natural barrier.
Conditionerr: A high-performance, protein-enriched formula that restores moisture balance, strengthens weakened strands, and smooths the cuticle.Frizz Fighting Hair Serum: A deeply reparative leave-in that hydrates, smooths, and protects against heat and environmental stressors

Cheat sheet: 

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: damage, heat styling, colour treatment, chemical straightening, protein treatment, smoothening treatment, straightening treatment. 
2. Do not recommend if the customer has natural hair that has never been chemically treated and doesnt heat style frequently
3. If a customer asks why their hair feels weighed down after using this routine, check whether they are over-applying the serum or conditioner or using it on damp / dry hair, all products are supposed to be used on wet hair

Moxie's product recommendation:
Moxie Ditch the Frizz Trio consisting of:
1. Gentle Cleansing Shampoo
2. Ultra Hydraitng Conditioner
3. Frizz Fighting Hair Serum
Comments:
The DTFT routine is recommended for people with fine hair who want to reduce frizz and enhance their natural texture. It is not suitable for those with thicker or coarser hair, as the lightweight formulations may not provide enough moisture for their needs.

Why fine hair specifically: Heavy or overly moisturizing products weigh fine hair down, causing it to fall flat and feel limp rather than bouncy and defined. Every product in the DTFT routine is intentionally lightweight to avoid this, giving fine hair the right amount of moisture without sacrificing volume or texture.

Shampoo: Gently cleanses product buildup and impurities from the scalp without stripping or being harsh on the hair strands.
Conditionerr: A thick, mask-like conditioner formulated with shea butter. Deeply conditions and softens hair, delivering rich hydration during the wash.
Frizz Fighting Hair Serum: A lightweight, milky serum applied after washing. Targets the root cause of frizz (dryness) by deeply hydrating the hair shaft without weighing it down or leaving residue.

Cheat sheet: 

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: frizz, fine hair, flat hair, limp hair, or wanting to enhance natural texture.
2. Do not recommend if the customer has thick, coarse, or low-porosity hair that needs heavier moisture.
3. If a customer asks why their hair feels weighed down after using this routine, check whether they are over-applying the serum or conditioner or using it on damp / dry hair, all products are supposed to be used on wet hair

Moxie's product recommendation:
A. Hydrorepair Routine:
1. Hyaluronic acid shampoo
2. Hyaluronic acd conditioner
3. Hyaluronic acid serum
Comments:
The HydroRepair routine is recommended for people with hair that has been damaged through excessive chemical treatments, colouring or heatstyling and they want to reduce frizz and enhance their natural texture and make hair more hydrated and treat damage. It is not suitable for those with virgin hair or people who have never gotten chemical treatments to their hair and don't heat style frequently, as the formulation may not work for them and might weigh their hair down.

Why chemically treated or frequently heat styled hair specifically: The hydrorepair routine is powered by hyaluronic acid and bioactive peptides. The hyaluronic acid reactivates the hydration pathways while the bioactive peptides rebuild the hair's lipid barrier making jhair hydrated and stronger. 

Hyaluronic Acid Repairing Shampoo: Gently cleanses while replenishing lost moisture and reinforcing your hair’s natural barrier.
Conditionerr: A high-performance, protein-enriched formula that restores moisture balance, strengthens weakened strands, and smooths the cuticle.Frizz Fighting Hair Serum: A deeply reparative leave-in that hydrates, smooths, and protects against heat and environmental stressors

Cheat sheet: 

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: damage, heat styling, colour treatment, chemical straightening, protein treatment, smoothening treatment, straightening treatment. 
2. Do not recommend if the customer has natural hair that has never been chemically treated and doesnt heat style frequently
3. If a customer asks why their hair feels weighed down after using this routine, check whether they are over-applying the serum or conditioner or using it on damp / dry hair, all products are supposed to be used on wet hair

Moxie's product recommendation:
Moxie Ditch the Frizz Trio consisting of:
1. Gentle Cleansing Shampoo
2. Ultra Hydraitng Conditioner
3. Frizz Fighting Hair Serum
Comments:
The DTFT routine is recommended for people with fine hair who want to reduce frizz and enhance their natural texture. It is not suitable for those with thicker or coarser hair, as the lightweight formulations may not provide enough moisture for their needs.

Why fine hair specifically: Heavy or overly moisturizing products weigh fine hair down, causing it to fall flat and feel limp rather than bouncy and defined. Every product in the DTFT routine is intentionally lightweight to avoid this, giving fine hair the right amount of moisture without sacrificing volume or texture.

Shampoo: Gently cleanses product buildup and impurities from the scalp without stripping or being harsh on the hair strands.
Conditionerr: A thick, mask-like conditioner formulated with shea butter. Deeply conditions and softens hair, delivering rich hydration during the wash.
Frizz Fighting Hair Serum: A lightweight, milky serum applied after washing. Targets the root cause of frizz (dryness) by deeply hydrating the hair shaft without weighing it down or leaving residue.

Cheat sheet: 

1. Recommend this routine when a customer mentions: frizz, fine hair, flat hair, limp hair, or wanting to enhance natural texture.
2. Do not recommend if the customer has thick, coarse, or low-porosity hair that needs heavier moisture.
3. If a customer asks why their hair feels weighed down after using this routine, check whether they are over-applying the serum or conditioner or using it on damp / dry hair, all products are supposed to be used on wet hair
